whatToFeed: Balanced organic flower fertilizer or a low-nitrogen mix paired with a tomato-style feed to encourage heavy spring and summer blooming cycles.
pruningTips: Depends heavily on the specific pruning group. For standard Group 3 summer bloomers, cut all canes down hard to about 12 inches above the ground in late winter or early spring to stimulate vigorous, flowering new growth.
flavorProfile: Not edible. Mildly toxic to humans and household pets.
combativePlants: Shade trees that block lower vine light
whenToPlant: Zone 6/7: Plant container starts outdoors in spring (mid-to-late April) or early autumn (September) when the soil is cool and workable.
whenToHarvest: Cut fully opened flower stems on a diagonal for stunning, long-lasting summer indoor arrangements.
usesForLargeHarvests: Training up garden obelisks or classic wooden trellises, covering open fences, or creating high-impact vertical floral backdrops.
hardinessNote: Extremely hardy perennial vine; the key to success is keeping its top growth in the bright sun while keeping its root system shaded and cool.
plantingInstructions: Dig a hole 2 inches deeper than the container nursery soil level. Plant the crown slightly below the soil line to stimulate secondary shoots from the base. Mulch heavily or place a flat stone over the root zone immediately to keep the soil surface cool.
growthRequirements: Demands deep, rich, fertile, and highly moist clay-loam or loam soils with exceptional drainage and a neutral pH of 6.5 to 7.0. Requires structural support like wire mesh, twine, or a trellis framework to climb. Prefers roots in the shade, tops in the sun.
